_rationale: find_files with pattern='*.go' on multi-lang corpus returns the fuzzy-match file list — all 4 corpus files match because '*.go' is fuzzy-scored against absolute paths (every file gets score 0.574). Go and C++ emit byte-identical 734-byte payloads after the runner's normalize_mcp_inner_text canonicalises array order and rewrites corpus paths. Retrofitted with full _rationale per iter-7 karpathy rule (was boilerplate from pre-rationale era). Probe: /tmp/parity-iter4/find_files___pattern_____go.{go,cpp}.json both 734 bytes.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope matches MCP spec. result.content[].text byte-for-byte identical after the inner_text normaliser sorts the `results` array (sort_array_paths) and rewrites the corpus prefix. The four matched paths, scores, match_type='fuzzy', and total_matches=4 are all stable. results[].file_id is ignored by the normaliser since file IDs are session-dependent — not listed in the descriptor's ignore tier because the inner_text normaliser strips it during canonicalisation (parity_runner.cpp:82-88).
tiers._rationale_ignore: Universal MCP descriptor noise (id/jsonrpc) and timing/pid fields the runner injects.
